session_summary: This 2026-06-01 session derived the closed-form general-MVN (A)+(C) Term-A asymptote, with the hard ingredient being the second-order FIML bias of the covariance under MAR. Marcus's role was to set the agenda (literature-first, don't reinvent the wheel), gate the derivation through a plan, steer the work to manuscript form, probe method choices (why finite differencing when analytic Hessians exist), handle Zotero acquisitions himself, and request a clean handoff prompt for the next session. Much of the human-side traffic is short scheduling/status checks while long ampute-bound Monte Carlo runs completed in the background.

coded_turns.quote: BEFORE deriving b_Σ from scratch, SEARCH THE LITERATURE — the MVN-under-missing-data MLE bias is a\n  classical problem and I do not want to reinvent the wheel.
coded_turns.note: Kickoff: literature-first gate before any derivation, plan-mode hold, monotone-vs-general scope to resolve in plan.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/010 handoff; b_Sigma literature pass logged in todo/003

coded_turns.quote: <task-notification>
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: the literature-pass subagent returned its reuse-vs-derive verdict (Little&Rubin partial-reuse).

coded_turns.quote: Background command \"Run lean (A)+(C) asymptote evaluator across N\" completed
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: background (A)+(C) asymptote evaluator across N finished.

coded_turns.quote: Background command \"Measure b_Sigma directly at R=40000 (validate hard ingredient)\" completed
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: direct b_Sigma measurement run finished.

coded_turns.quote: Do you have a sense of how much longer this is gonna take?
coded_turns.note: Time check on the long high-R curve run; light scheduling pressure, no substantive direction.

coded_turns.quote: Background command \"High-R measured (A)+(C) curve at large N to pin asymptote\" completed
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: high-R large-N measured curve finished (scatters ~-0.20).

coded_turns.quote: Give me a table of what you predicted and what it showed
coded_turns.note: Demands a prediction-vs-measured comparison table, forcing the closed form to be checked against the simulation.

coded_turns.quote: Okay so what’s next?
coded_turns.note: Asks for the next step after the verification table; hands pacing back to the AI.

coded_turns.quote: Did I already add those to zotero can you check?
coded_turns.note: Checks the literature/acquisition record before re-running pipeline for Hyodo / Kanda-Fujikoshi.

coded_turns.quote: Give me the author and title
coded_turns.note: Requests author/title of the references so he can add them to Zotero himself.

coded_turns.quote: Go ahead with path b
coded_turns.note: Approves the analytic exact-constant route (path b) over more Monte Carlo.

coded_turns.quote: How long will this take?
coded_turns.note: Duration check on the path-b run; scheduling, no substantive steer.

coded_turns.quote: Let's get it in manuscript form.
coded_turns.note: Steers from verification to writing up the Term-A result in derivation.qmd, accepting monotone scope.

coded_turns.quote: Background command \"Render derivation.qmd to confirm clean build\" completed
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: derivation.qmd render succeeded (clean build).

coded_turns.quote: Why is the MCAR taking so long?
coded_turns.note: Probes why the MCAR control run is slow; opens the cost question answered in the next turn.

coded_turns.quote: Why are we doing a finite differentiation? I thought we had analytic Hessians available to us.
coded_turns.note: Catches an avoidable finite-diff verification cost; pushes to swap in the analytic H_phi, which the AI then derives.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: analytic H_phi (Gauss-Newton + Schur) in derivation.qmd sec-termA

coded_turns.quote: Background command \"Validate analytic H_phi vs finite-diff\" completed
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: analytic-vs-finite-diff H_phi validation run finished.

coded_turns.quote: Background command \"Re-validate analytic H_phi with block-wise diagnostics\" completed
coded_turns.note: System task-notification: block-wise H_phi re-validation finished (mu-mu block check).

coded_turns.quote: Okay, there are pdfs that need to be migrated
coded_turns.note: Signals he is handling the reference-PDF migration for the pending citations on his side.

coded_turns.quote: Alright, I am deal with the literature now. What is the next step after the literature is all set?
coded_turns.note: Splits work: he takes the literature, asks the AI to sequence post-acquisition steps.

coded_turns.quote: Go ahead an make a markdown that I can bring to a new session. I'm working on the literature in another session so we can assume that they will be indexed, but that the markdown versions will not appear and the lit sync tool will need to be invoked
coded_turns.note: Orders a handoff markdown and specifies the cross-session state: refs indexed but lit-sync still required.

coded_turns.quote: give me the prompt
coded_turns.note: Requests the paste-ready resume prompt for the next session.

ai_side.evidence: Two things only you can move:\n- **Acquisitions** ... - **Commit** ... My recommendation: **let me start path b now**
ai_side.note: AI laid out the user-gated options (acquisitions, commit) plus a recommended next move.

ai_side.evidence: Want me to continue through the reductions, or pause here with the result?
ai_side.note: AI surfaced the continue-vs-pause decision instead of deciding silently.

ai_side.evidence: the **Hyodo** paper's headline is *shrinkage* covariance estimation — the MLE + unbiased-estimator pair we rely on is in its **Section 2** background
ai_side.note: AI flagged a risk that the needed result sits in background and may not survive Mathpix conversion, with a fallback.

ai_side.evidence: The two noise-limited checks (`b_Σ` direct, the large-N `(A)+(C)` curve) are *consistent* with the closed form (≈−0.24) but can't pin it tighter than the ~0.1–0.2 MCSE floor
ai_side.note: AI flagged that the Monte Carlo checks cannot pin the constant, motivating the analytic path b.

ai_side.evidence: `todo/012` is currently **uncommitted** — want me to commit it so it's in history for the new session, or leave it on disk for you to review first?
ai_side.note: AI surfaced the commit-now-vs-review decision to the user.
